finding: a psychological trait predicting openness to novel experiences showed strong psychometric properties and a consistent nomological network extending to creative and innovative outputs
a perceiver's level of action identification covaried with mind attribution; targets were assigned higher-level action descriptions and greater mental states when liked
reduced left frontal cortical activation decreased a postdecisional attitude-change effect, suggesting approach-oriented motivational processes mediate decision-consonant attitude shifting
satisfying domain-specific psychological threats (power for one party, moral image for another) through social exchange increased willingness to resolve conflict
awareness of temporal constraint on a meaningful event increased mixed emotional experience among participants
implicit and explicit attitude measures converged when task structural demands became similar, suggesting format differences rather than independent cognition explain weak correlations
increased effort to control automatic associations reduced temporal irregularity in response times, indicating effort attenuates nonrandom variability in task performance
threat to a social identity paradoxically increased physical distancing from an outgroup member, independent of explicit or implicit prejudice
stated preferences for partner attributes differed by participant sex, but when interacting with real potential partners, actual attraction showed no sex differences linked to those attributes
processing a persuasive message with reference to important values increased message elaboration and later resistance to counterargument, mediated by enhanced initial processing
depleted self-regulation resources increased preference for standpoint-consistent information, mediated by heightened commitment to one's own position
a goal-implementation mindset produced more extreme, less ambivalent, and more accessible attitudes toward unrelated objects, predicting behavior better than expressed attitudes alone
high self-esteem individuals resolved competing self-protection and approach goals by prioritizing connectedness, while low self-esteem individuals prioritized protection when trust was required
perceived personal capability to commit a similar transgression increased forgiveness, mediated by reduced severity judgment and increased similarity perception
nonverbal expression of a pride-related emotion was reliably recognized across diverse cultures and ethnic groups, suggesting universal rather than culturally transmitted recognition
coherence between conceptual and embodied affective information improved cognitive recall, while incoherence impaired it
global processing style produced assimilation of information into judgment categories, while local processing produced contrast
communicated emotional expression signaled negotiating positions, with effects contingent on structural bargaining features; emotion strategy backfired under certain conditions
cues signaling low minority representation and colorblind diversity philosophy were perceived as identity threats, reducing trust among stigmatized group members
a person's own felt responsiveness toward a partner biased perception of the partner's reciprocal responsiveness, which guided relationship promotion behavior
